C[shapenaji: Hey! Thanks! Have a good game!
C[shapenaji: Hey! Thanks! Have a good game!
C[Uberdude: This game was a nice example of the advantages of omitting the white g2 black h2 exchange, keeping the aji of h2.

C[Uberdude: I've never seen this move before, and neither has GoGoD (though there are a few of it with a pre-existing black pincer). I could't find an amazing result for white so I don't think it can really be called a mistake, but I don't think it's that great either. Traditional q5 or r4 both look possible to me, probably q5 best.

C[Uberdude: Going into the corner directly didn't seem to work well so I just dodged here, planning on r12 if black takes the corner at r4. Then it's like joseki but black chose to play p6 rather than r11 checking extension, though of course maybe white would have chosen q10 rather than r9 had black r4 directly (I hadn't decided).
C[Uberdude: s3 seemed a bit soft so I wanted q4 p4 e3 p3 r4 for a big strong corner.

C[Uberdude: Is this too much? I thought black o3, white s2 and then black takes sente for f17 or k17 or o17 or whatever big move he likes would be the calm way to play. That way black can expect to get at least 20 points of territory on the lower side, though  I would also be happy with my settled group on the right (r11 loses its attacking meaning and would be better at r14) and nice yose.
C[Uberdude: Always nice to make empty triangles.
C[Uberdude: I expected black to nobi one more time: he can't kill and still has to come back to live, but then white doesn't come out in such nice style.

C[Uberdude: This kind of reverse sente hanging connection to make shape is one that I've noticed in pro games. I worried that it might be slow but I think it's good to almost live, aim at h2, allow the l7 jump without a cut and threaten to kill at t3.

C[Uberdude: So comparing this result with the calm way of black defending the p3 cut and allowing white s2, black has no territory on the lower side and a few points in the corner. White has 2 weakish groups (l2 group actually seems the stronger to me, r11 is working well) and black 1. As black's joseki choice in the lower left corner gave up a lot of territory in exchange for thickness, I think white is leading unless black can find a way to profit by attacking, but it is far from clear who is attacking who as the j4 group is weak if white gets h2.

C[Uberdude: It was hard to find the right shape to come out here. This shape seems to have a problem at q8 but white can block at r8 and if black pushes and cuts white ataris through at q7 and ends up killing black.
C[Uberdude: I was concerned about black turning at m6 and pushing there a bit: if I keep on answering then q8 becomes a problem.

C[Uberdude: It was a shame to have to strengthen black here and give up hope of invading in the r14 area, but I thought I needed to to help my group. You could say black has transferred his moyo from the lower side to the upper right. But it's smaller and open at 3-3.
C[Uberdude: I expected black q12 immediately, but I didn't think I could tenuki this. Maybe f17 q12 c14 e15 c17 r13 is playable.
C[Uberdude: If black just took the corner I wasn't sure what to do. Probably q12 in sente and then maybe h2 or l7. It's not so easy to make points on the top side. Probably I go to r17 soon.

C[Uberdude: Joseki is h17, then white e14, I really liked this result for me as I got lots of points and thickness and a strong group on the top side which was the only place black could hope to make lots of points.  A blunder?

C[Uberdude: It seems h17 instead can make a small life and white does then have to worry about the f14 cut, but is happy enough to get strong influence. Maybe tenuki is best until black knows what to do here.
C[Uberdude: Huge gote move to prevent 3-3 invasion and a nice efficient shape with q12.
Uberdude: If black had helped the k17 stones at n17 or m16 then 3-3 would be perfect for white.

C[Uberdude: I did consider attacking the 2 stones at m16 or so, but decided they were small (and if black plays there it's open underneath anyway) so took this key point to help my group come out and aim at attacking the black groups to left and right.
C[Uberdude: Needed to defend against n8 cut.

C[Uberdude: What is the purpose of this move? Separate and attack l2 group? That seems rather unlikely as white has already spent a move to come out at l7. It doesn't even settle black, whereas g2 would. Seems more likely to end up as dame.
C[Uberdude: K2 successfully turned into dame and black's base removed.
C[Uberdude: This was a huge surprise: I thought running out at n11 was the only move.
Uberdude: Seems rather greedy leaving 2 weak groups.
C[Uberdude: This prevents black making at eye with m8 and o8 (and was a time tesuji).
C[Uberdude: Despite black omitting n11 I couldn't find a way to kill that group: n11 is a nice point and forces black out at m10, but there is a weakness with o10 and q8 peeps and the q10 cut
column2: while m10 almost seals with the m11 clamp if black o11, but black has enough aji to break out. So decided to attack this one first. It was a shame I couldn't get the g6 peep (black resists at h6).

column1: C[Uberdude: This ponnuki is a bit overconcentrated with l9.

C[Uberdude: The plan with the ponnuki was to then kill with m10, but I chickened out as m10 ends up with m10 o11 n11 o10 o12 and then p12 cut has to be answered inside as q8 is sente for r8 cut, so then o12 gets chased towards the top with n12 o13 n13 o14 etc and I lose that semeai. So first I aimed to get some stones in sente at the top.
Uberdude: Another idea was n11 m10 l12 but black can live by pushing out and making his second eye at m8 as m7 is his sente.
C[Uberdude: Ignoring m16 to help this group made me sad, but I was happy black played this thin move as it leaves many problems for later. n11 would seem honte to me.
C[Uberdude: When I played this I thought k17 were dead, but the aji is quite bad so maybe n17 is safer, but this is much better yose and can also aim at r18 invasion later.
Uberdude: e8 is also a powerful move to attack the f8 group, but I wanted to prepare the centre first.

C[Uberdude: This seems like another very greedy overplay move like k2: after I have spent a move in an area so that I will have the advantage in a fight there, black decides to fight there. Or is it punishing the o17 overplay, maybe my perspective favours white?
C[Uberdude: I get my own empty triangle to accompany p6.
C[Uberdude: I had thought j13 killed but it's messy. So I backed off and just went for this safe move as black has 3 weak groups and it aims to exploit the thinness of n11.

column2: I get the impression shapenaji likes to play creative unusual moves to demonstrate his strength, but simple j15 then j13 looks better.

column1: C[Uberdude: Seems a good answer to weird h14.

C[Uberdude: Maybe l13 would be sensible: let black live at n18 and then p12 with e8 to look forward to as white is far ahead even if black manages to save both groups (or loses c9 to save f8). But I didn't feel like backing down from a fight and I have loads of threats and don't really mind if I lose the ko anyway.
C[Uberdude: I think black has to p13 to keep some pressure on that white group.
C[Uberdude: More overplay, but then if black helps at l12 or k11 then it's too simple for white so I suppose black has to look for complications or else resign.
C[Uberdude: Better to o14 or n13 first before making white stronger.
C[Uberdude: Now e8 is even more serious.
C[Uberdude: Black doesn't have enough threats so I wasn't afraid of the ko even though it looks dangerous for white now.
C[Uberdude: Black should e18 to make more threats. It also makes g13 a better threat if white can't connect under in such good shape/at all.
Uberdude: Scratch that, too late for e18, it was possible earlier. f18 perhaps.
C[Uberdude: Black has a 100% sente local threat at g19. g13 I'll ignore.
Uberdude: As p13 wasn't answered I have r14 so this can capture the r7 stones but then I'll live by capturing r12.
C[Uberdude: o10 directly is better, I can't answer or black pokes out my eyes in sente, so then afterwards black's follow-up is the q10 cut to capture q11 stones in the yose rather than p9.
C[Uberdude: All in! But not enough threats again.
C[Uberdude: I'm not sure I actually need to answer this, but decided to play it safe and only go for 50 rather than 100 point win
C[Uberdude: Playing it safe again, black can't live inside but ko liberties are annoying in a semeai.
C[Uberdude: I think I do actually have enough threats to answer this (and e2 d1 e4) at s14 p15 o11 p7 o5 but wanted to kill the lower right corner rather than connect on dame at p14.

Uberdude: Also I was disappointed never to get the chance to attack at e8, but if I answer this later threats are bigger and maybe black finds more threats in the corner I didn't expect so ending the ko gives a safe 50 point win.
C[Uberdude: Oh noes! I lost some yose...
